sections.heading: RT
sections.content: {"role": "Radiation therapy (RT) is used in the adjuvant setting for resected disease with positive margins or node-positive disease, for unresectable disease in combination with chemotherapy, and for palliation of symptomatic primary or metastatic lesions.", "principles": ["RT dosing is based on the ability to meet normal organ constraints and underlying liver function.", "Image-guided RT (IGRT) is strongly recommended when using RT, IMRT, and SBRT to improve accuracy and reduce toxicity.", "Concurrent chemoradiation should be limited to 5-fluorouracil or capecitabine; concurrent gemcitabine is not recommended due to toxicity.", "RT can be considered for all tumor locations if organ constraints are met.", "Brachytherapy may be considered at experienced centers."], "dose_frameworks": [{"name": "Stereotactic Body Radiation Therapy (SBRT)", "total_dose": "40-60 Gy", "dose_per_fraction": "8-20 Gy", "fractions": "3-5", "schedule": "Over 1-2 weeks", "indication": "Unresectable intrahepatic or extrahepatic CCA; BED10 >100 Gy preferred if constraints allow"}, {"name": "Hypofractionated RT", "total_dose": "58-67.5 Gy", "dose_per_fraction": "3.9-4.5 Gy", "fractions": "15", "schedule": "Over 3 weeks", "indication": "Unresectable disease at centers with experience; photons or protons"}, {"name": "Conventional Fractionation RT", "total_dose": "60-77 Gy", "dose_per_fraction": "1.8-2 Gy", "fractions": "30-35", "schedule": "Over 6-7 weeks", "indication": "If SBRT/hypofractionation not feasible; may be combined with chemotherapy"}, {"name": "Adjuvant RT", "total_dose": "45 Gy to regional nodes; 50-60 Gy to tumor bed", "dose_per_fraction": "1.8-2 Gy", "fractions": "25-33", "schedule": "Over 5-6.5 weeks", "indication": "Resected eCCA or gallbladder cancer with positive margins or high-risk features"}], "approaches": [{"name": "Concurrent Chemoradiation (Capecitabine or 5-FU)", "dose_fractionation": "Up to 60 Gy in 30 fractions", "concurrent_chemotherapy": "Capecitabine or 5-fluorouracil", "indication": "Unresectable disease; adjuvant setting for high-risk features", "key_trial": "SWOG S0809 (adjuvant setting)", "toxicities": "Nausea, diarrhea, myelosuppression, hepatotoxicity"}, {"name": "Palliative Radiation Therapy", "dose_fractionation": "Various short courses (e.g., 30 Gy in 10 fractions)", "concurrent_chemotherapy": "None", "indication": "Symptom control for primary tumor, bone metastases, brain metastases", "key_trial": null, "toxicities": "Depends on site treated"}]}

timeline.title: Stereotactic Body Radiation Therapy (SBRT)
timeline.body: Total dose: 40-60 Gy, Dose per fraction: 8-20 Gy, Fractions: 3-5, Schedule: Over 1-2 weeks, Indication: Unresectable intrahepatic or extrahepatic CCA; BED10 >100 Gy preferred if constraints allow

timeline.title: Hypofractionated RT
timeline.body: Total dose: 58-67.5 Gy, Dose per fraction: 3.9-4.5 Gy, Fractions: 15, Schedule: Over 3 weeks, Indication: Unresectable disease at centers with experience; photons or protons

timeline.title: Conventional Fractionation RT
timeline.body: Total dose: 60-77 Gy, Dose per fraction: 1.8-2 Gy, Fractions: 30-35, Schedule: Over 6-7 weeks, Indication: If SBRT/hypofractionation not feasible; may be combined with chemotherapy

timeline.title: Adjuvant RT
timeline.body: Total dose: 45 Gy to regional nodes; 50-60 Gy to tumor bed, Dose per fraction: 1.8-2 Gy, Fractions: 25-33, Schedule: Over 5-6.5 weeks, Indication: Resected eCCA or gallbladder cancer with positive margins or high-risk features

timeline.title: Concurrent Chemoradiation (Capecitabine or 5-FU)
timeline.body: Dose fractionation: Up to 60 Gy in 30 fractions, Concurrent chemotherapy: Capecitabine or 5-fluorouracil, Indication: Unresectable disease; adjuvant setting for high-risk features, Key trial: SWOG S0809 (adjuvant setting), Toxicities: Nausea, diarrhea, myelosuppression, hepatotoxicity

timeline.title: Palliative Radiation Therapy
timeline.body: Dose fractionation: Various short courses (e.g., 30 Gy in 10 fractions), Concurrent chemotherapy: None, Indication: Symptom control for primary tumor, bone metastases, brain metastases, Toxicities: Depends on site treated
